steps.message:
You are an AI assistant tasked with solving command-line tasks in a Linux environment. You will be given a task description and the output from previously executed commands. Your goal is to solve the task by providing batches of shell commands.

Format your response as JSON with the following structure:

{
  "analysis": "Analyze the current state based on the terminal output provided. What do you see? What has been accomplished? What still needs to be done?",
  "plan": "Describe your plan for the next steps. What commands will you run and why? Be specific about what you expect each command to accomplish.",
  "commands": [
    {
      "keystrokes": "ls -la\n",
      "duration": 0.1
    },
    {
      "keystrokes": "cd project\n",
      "duration": 0.1
    }
  ],
  "task_complete": true
}

Required fields:
- "analysis": Your analysis of the current situation
- "plan": Your plan for the next steps
- "commands": Array of command objects to execute

Optional fields:
- "task_complete": Boolean indicating if the task is complete (defaults to false if not present)

Command object structure:
- "keystrokes": String containing the exact keystrokes to send to the terminal (required)
- "duration": Number of seconds to wait for the command to complete before the next command will be executed (defaults to 1.0 if not present)

IMPORTANT: The text inside "keystrokes" will be used completely verbatim as keystrokes. Write commands exactly as you want them sent to the terminal:
- You must end every command with a newline (\n) or it will not execute.
- For special key sequences, use tmux-style escape sequences:
  - C-c for Ctrl+C
  - C-d for Ctrl+D

The "duration" attribute specifies the number of seconds to wait for the command to complete (default: 1.0) before the next command will be executed. On immediate tasks (e.g., cd, ls, echo, cat) set a duration of 0.1 seconds. On commands (e.g., gcc, find, rustc) set a duration of 1.0 seconds. On slow commands (e.g., make, python3 [long running script], wget [file]) set an appropriate duration as you determine necessary.

It is better to set a smaller duration than a longer duration. It is always possible to wait again if the prior output has not finished, by running {"keystrokes": "", "duration": 10.0} on subsequent requests to wait longer. Never wait longer than 60 seconds; prefer to poll to see intermediate result status.

Important notes:
- Each command's keystrokes are sent exactly as written to the terminal
- Do not include extra whitespace before or after the keystrokes unless it's part of the intended command
- Extra text before or after the JSON will generate warnings but be tolerated
- The JSON must be valid - use proper escaping for quotes and special characters within strings
- Commands array can be empty if you want to wait without taking action

Task Description:
Build a React single-page Hashi (Bridges) puzzle game with a retro terminal look: black background, green monospace text, no CSS frameworks. Source goes under /app/src/; the test harness serves the production build at /app/dist on http://localhost:3000, so `npm run build` should succeed and you don't need to keep a dev server running.

clicking island A then island B cycles the bridge between them through none → single → double → none. Right-click or Ctrl+click on an existing bridge removes it entirely (single or double, both go to none). Arrow keys move a visible cursor between islands; Enter selects an island, arrow keys move toward another, Enter toggles the bridge; Esc cancels. Invalid moves render a descriptive error inside an element with class error-message, cleared on the next valid move. The error text must be one of: "Cannot connect non-adjacent islands", "Max 2 bridges per pair", "Island would exceed its number", "Bridges would cross".

The board is a single SVG of 800×600 anchored to the viewport top-left — no centering, no page padding. Each grid unit is 40px, so an island at grid (x, y) renders at pixel (x*40, y*40). Islands are SVG circle elements; bridges are line elements (two parallel lines for a double). The keyboard cursor must be clearly visible but must not be a circle — use rect, stroke filter, group transform, anything else — so the board's circle count always equals the island count.

Islands are numbered circles with values 1–8; the number is the exact bridge count required at that island. Bridges are straight horizontal or vertical lines between two islands, max two per pair, never crossing. The puzzle is solved when every island's count is satisfied and all islands form one connected component.

Puzzle data lives at /app/src/data/puzzles.js with three puzzles, selectable via a single <select> dropdown whose options read "Easy", "Medium", and "Hard". Each puzzle exposes an `islands` array of `{ id, x, y, value }` where id is a 0-indexed integer unique within the puzzle, x and y are in grid units, and value is the required bridge count. Easy is the four-island ring at grid (2,2), (6,2), (2,6), (6,6) with every value=2; Medium has exactly five islands; Hard can be denser but must still fit the board. A Reset button clears all bridges on the current puzzle while leaving the islands in place. When the puzzle is solved, render a banner with class victory-banner containing the text SOLVED!.

One thing worth flagging from prototyping: the error-message and victory-banner only need to exist while their condition is active, and the dropdown handler should fully reset bridges when the puzzle changes. The first mouse click on an island is just selection — the toggle runs on the second click against a different island, and once that toggle fires the selection clears so the next click starts a fresh pair. Resist the urge to layer invisible or wider `<line>` elements over the visible bridge for easier click detection: the total `<line>` count is observable and should equal the visible bridge count (one per single, two per double).

Game logic lives at /app/src/utils/hashiLogic.js. Bridges are stored as Map<string, number> keyed by the canonical "x1,y1,x2,y2" string where the endpoint with the smaller (x, then y) sorts first; coordinates in the key are grid units. canAddBridge(islands, bridges, from, to, delta) returning { valid, error } where error, when non-null, is one of the four strings above; computeCurrentCounts(islands, bridges) returning Map<islandId, number> keyed by the island's numeric id (handle id=0 carefully); checkConnectivity(islands, bridges) returning whether every island is reachable from every other; isVictory(islands, bridges) returning whether every island's count equals its value, no bridges cross, and the graph is connected.



Current terminal state:
Current Terminal Screen:
root@b5a203f9b7ef:/app#

Analysis: No commands have been run yet. Need to inspect the existing React/Vite project structure under /app, then implement required files: /app/src/data/puzzles.js, /app/src/utils/hashiLogic.js, and the React UI (single 800x600 SVG, retro terminal styling, mouse/keyboard controls, error/victory rendering). Finally ensure npm run build succeeds and outputs to /app/dist.
Plan: 1) Inspect repository and existing build tooling (package.json, src structure). 2) Open current src entry files to understand existing app scaffolding. 3) Then implement puzzles.js and hashiLogic.js per spec, and update React components/styles accordingly. For now, run filesystem and file content discovery commands.

Analysis: Vite+React project exists with minimal App. src/data/puzzles.js already present with 3 puzzles, but Medium/Hard may not match required constraints exactly (Easy matches). Need to add /src/utils/hashiLogic.js with required functions and bridge Map keying. Need to implement full UI in App.jsx: 800x600 SVG anchored top-left, retro styling, select dropdown (Easy/Medium/Hard) that resets bridges on change, Reset button, error-message and victory-banner conditional rendering, mouse interactions (click to select then second click toggles), right-click/Ctrl+click removes bridge, keyboard cursor (not a circle) with arrow navigation and Enter toggling. Must ensure line counts equal visible lines (no invisible overlays). Must implement crossing rules, adjacency, max 2, island counts, connectivity, victory. Finally run npm run build.
Plan: 1) Create src/utils/hashiLogic.js implementing: canonical bridge key, adjacency checks (same row/col with no island between), crossing detection, canAddBridge, computeCurrentCounts, checkConnectivity, isVictory. 2) Update puzzles.js so Medium has exactly five islands (already) and ensure Hard fits board and is denser (ok) while options names exactly Easy/Medium/Hard. 3) Implement App.jsx to render controls + SVG board, manage state: selected puzzle, bridges Map, selected island id (mouse selection), keyboard cursor island id and keyboard selection state, error message. Implement handlers for click, contextmenu, keydown. Render islands as circles + text; bridges as 1 or 2 SVG lines with offsets for doubles; cursor as rect highlight. 4) Ensure CSS keeps top-left anchoring and no centering/padding; style buttons/select in terminal theme. 5) Build to verify.

Analysis: Build command started but we didn't see completion output yet (likely still running or output truncated). Need to wait for build to finish and, if it fails, inspect errors. Also need to ensure hashiLogic.js and App.jsx files were fully written (earlier output was truncated mid-file display, so we should verify file integrity). Finally verify puzzle constraints and line/circle counts behavior; adjust logic for right-click removal (should remove existing bridge regardless of current selection per spec) and ensure SVG is anchored top-left with no extra padding/centering (UI bar currently adds padding above SVG; spec wants board anchored to viewport top-left—may require moving controls outside SVG area but still top-left; however anchored means SVG at 0,0. We'll adjust if needed after confirming tests expectations).
Plan: 1) Poll for npm build completion. 2) If build failed, read the error and fix. 3) Verify contents of src/utils/hashiLogic.js and src/App.jsx are complete/valid. 4) If build succeeded, run a quick grep to ensure required strings/classes exist. 5) Make any necessary spec corrections (especially right-click behavior and SVG at exact top-left).

arguments.keystrokes: grep -R "victory-banner\|error-message\|SOLVED!\|Cannot connect non-adjacent islands\|Max 2 bridges per pair\|Island would exceed its number\|Bridges would cross" -n /app/src | head

steps.message:
Analysis: Production build succeeded (exit code 0, dist generated). Need to fix two issues: (1) previous command batch had an empty keystrokes without newline causing concatenation warning—avoid that going forward. (2) grep command failed due to history expansion on ! in SOLVED!; must escape/quote or disable histexp. Also need to re-check spec compliance: SVG must be anchored at viewport top-left with no padding/centering; current UI bar adds padding above SVG so SVG is not at (0,0). Likely need to move controls into an overlay positioned absolute (or inside SVG) but requirement says board is single SVG anchored top-left; easiest: make SVG positioned fixed at top-left (0,0) and render controls below/right using absolute overlay not affecting SVG position. Also right-click behavior: spec says right-click or Ctrl+click on an existing bridge removes it entirely; current implementation requires selecting endpoints. Need to allow removal by interacting with bridge lines themselves (click on line) without adding extra invisible lines; can attach onContextMenu/onClick handlers directly to visible lines. That preserves line count. We'll implement: left-click on a bridge line cycles? spec only says island clicks cycle; for removal, right-click/Ctrl+click on an existing bridge removes. We'll add onContextMenu and onClick with ctrlKey on line(s) to remove bridge key. Need mapping from rendered line back to bridge key; we have it in loop. Also remove counts-hint (not required but okay). Finally rerun npm run build.
Plan: 1) Update App.jsx: make SVG fixed at top-left with no surrounding padding; move UI into absolutely positioned overlay that does not shift SVG (e.g., position: fixed; top:0; left:810px or top:610px). But simplest: keep UI as overlay within a fixed container while SVG remains fixed at (0,0). 2) Add event handlers on visible bridge <line> elements: onContextMenu preventDefault and remove that bridge; onClick if ctrlKey then remove. Ensure both lines of a double remove the bridge. Clear error on valid removal. 3) Fix CSS accordingly. 4) Rebuild. 5) Run grep with set +H to disable history expansion.

Analysis: Latest build succeeded. However, the App.jsx here-doc entry got visibly corrupted during input (random fragments like "d(null);" and "function onKeyDown(e" appearing mid-stream), which suggests the file likely contains syntax errors even though vite build succeeded (it might have used cached previous file? but build succeeded after writing, so we must verify App.jsx content is clean). Need to open App.jsx fully to ensure no stray text. Also ensure hashiLogic.js complete (earlier truncation). Finally ensure required error strings are exactly and only those four, and that invalid moves clear on next valid move (we do). We'll verify file with eslint/node parse by running node with a JSX parser isn't available; instead just inspect suspicious sections via sed/grep for corrupted tokens.
Plan: 1) Inspect App.jsx around the corrupted-looking sections to confirm actual file contents. 2) If corrupted, rewrite App.jsx cleanly (again) and rebuild. 3) Quick check hashiLogic.js tail for completeness and exported functions. 4) Final build to confirm.

Analysis: We can see the end of hashiLogic.js in the terminal; it looks complete and exports isVictory and checkConnectivity properly. Still need to inspect App.jsx for corruption; previous nl/sed commands were planned but not executed yet. Next step: print App.jsx with line numbers to confirm it's valid and contains no stray fragments. If it is corrupted, rewrite cleanly and rebuild.
Plan: Run nl+sed on App.jsx in three chunks to inspect entire file for accidental inserted text. If any corruption is found, overwrite App.jsx with the intended clean version and run npm run build again.

Analysis: App.jsx appears intact at least in the displayed tail section; no visible corruption there. Since build succeeded and file tail looks correct, likely the earlier weird fragments were just terminal display artifacts during here-doc input, not actually written. Remaining tasks: ensure grep for required error strings exists in hashiLogic.js, ensure puzzles.js meets specs (Easy ring; Medium exactly five islands already; Hard fits). Also ensure right-click/Ctrl+click removal on bridge lines is implemented (now yes). Need to ensure dropdown change fully resets bridges (yes). Finally, run one more build (already succeeded) and do quick string checks for the four error messages and that SVG is 800x600 anchored top-left. Then mark complete.
Plan: 1) Verify App.jsx header/middle quickly for syntax sanity and presence of single SVG 800x600. 2) Verify hashiLogic.js exports required functions and includes exact error strings. 3) Verify puzzles.js Easy/Medium/Hard match constraints. 4) Run npm run build one last time to confirm clean. Then finish.

arguments.keystrokes: grep -R "Cannot connect non-adjacent islands\|Max 2 bridges per pair\|Island would exceed its number\|Bridges would cross" -n /app/src/utils/hashiLogic.js

steps.message:
Analysis: Verified App.jsx contains a single <svg> sized 800x600. Verified hashiLogic.js defines the four required error strings exactly. Verified puzzles.js contains Easy (correct 4-island ring), Medium has exactly five islands, and Hard fits the board. Build was re-run; we need to wait for its completion output to ensure it finishes successfully, then we can confidently mark complete.
Plan: Wait briefly for the ongoing `npm run build` to finish and confirm it exits successfully (no errors).
